summary: Small item decomp PR for SeaKchain that implemented it_2725_Logic54_PickedUp and corrected nearby callback return types. The implemented pickup logic gets the Item from the Item_GObj, pads the stack, starts an item animation update via Item_80268E5C, and installs fn_802BB428 as item->on_accessory. Review focused on removing unnecessary HSD_GObj/function-pointer casts by updating fn_802BB428's declaration and definition to the proper Item_GObj-compatible void callback type.
what_changed: Replaced the placeholder for it_2725_Logic54_PickedUp with a matching C implementation.
Implemented it_2725_Logic54_PickedUp(Item_GObj* gobj) to call GET_ITEM(gobj), include PAD_STACK(4), call Item_80268E5C(gobj, 0, ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE), and assign item->on_accessory = fn_802BB428.
Changed fn_802BB428 from returning s32 to returning void, both in itseakchain.c and itseakchain.h.
Changed fn_802BB428's body from returning it_802BCA30(GET_ITEM(gobj)) to calling it_802BCA30(GET_ITEM(gobj)) without using a return value.
Updated the header declaration for it_802BCA30 from s32 it_802BCA30(Item* item) to void it_802BCA30(Item* item).
Adjusted includes in itseakchain.c by removing itlinkhookshot.h and adding it/item.h.
smart_moves: Resolved callback assignment casts by making the callback signature match its actual use: fn_802BB428 is a void Item_GObj callback assigned directly to item->on_accessory.
Propagated the return-type correction into the header, avoiding mismatches between definition and declaration.
Preserved a PAD_STACK(4) in it_2725_Logic54_PickedUp, indicating the match required an explicit stack-padding tactic.
Used Item_80268E5C with ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE in the pickup routine, matching the item animation update path expected by this logic.
decomp_lessons: When a callback assignment requires casts, first check whether the callee's declaration and definition have the wrong return type or object type; fixing the signature may produce cleaner matching code.
For item accessory callbacks, prefer direct assignment to item->on_accessory when the function type is correctly declared, rather than hiding mismatches with casts.
If a wrapper function only calls another routine whose return value is unused in callback context, the wrapper and callee may need void return types rather than s32.
Header prototypes should be updated alongside source definitions during decomp cleanup, especially for functions used as callbacks.
Small stack-shape requirements may still need explicit PAD_STACK even in simple decompiled item routines.
naming_conventions: Kept existing address-based names such as fn_802BB428, it_802BCA30, it_802BCB88, and it_802BCED4 where semantic names were not established.
Used the explicit item logic name it_2725_Logic54_PickedUp for the implemented pickup handler.
Used project-standard item object types Item_GObj and Item, with GET_ITEM(gobj) for object-to-item access.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(4) in it_2725_Logic54_PickedUp to satisfy matching stack layout.
Removed casts from Item_80268E5C and item->on_accessory assignment after correcting fn_802BB428's signature.
Changed return-value-producing calls into void calls where the generated code only needed side effects.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Drop unnecessary casts in it_2725_Logic54_PickedUp. Suggested using Item_80268E5C(gobj, 0, ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE) and item->on_accessory = fn_802BB428 directly; noted that the declaration and definition of fn_802BB428 needed to be updated to match.

review_feedback.author: Gelatart
review_feedback.feedback: Responded that they tried making the requested changes.

follow_up_queries: Check surrounding SeaKchain functions still marked with /// # placeholders for further easy item decomp targets.
Search for other item->on_accessory assignments that still use casts and may indicate incorrect callback prototypes.
Verify other declarations of it_802BCA30, if any, agree with the updated void return type.
Look for similar pickup handlers using Item_80268E5C(gobj, 0, ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE) and PAD_STACK to reuse matching patterns.
